final_output.final_deliverable:
Report: attempted retrieval of San Francisco population from three authoritative sources (government, news outlet, research institution)

1) Mandate
- Find the current population of San Francisco as reported by three different authoritative sources (government, news outlet, and research institution). For each source, provide: the population figure, the date of the data, and a direct citation URL. Then identify discrepancies and state which figure is most likely accurate and why.

2) What I actually retrieved (summary of evidence available in this run)
- The search step located likely authoritative candidate URLs (for example, the U.S. Census QuickFacts page for San Francisco) but the subsequent visit steps in this run mistakenly fetched Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) pages instead of the San Francisco pages. Therefore, the raw page text available to quote verbatim (RAW VISIT CONTENT) contains ABS (Australia) material, not San Francisco population figures. Because of that, I do not have RAW_VISIT_CONTENT evidence that contains any San Francisco population numbers. I can list candidate authoritative URLs found in search/memory results, but I cannot claim a specific population number for San Francisco extracted from those pages because those pages were not visited in this run and they are not present in RAW VISIT CONTENT.

3) Evidence showing what was actually visited (verbatim quotes from RAW VISIT CONTENT)
- The run visited two ABS pages. Verbatim quotes from those pages (primary evidence in RAW VISIT CONTENT) demonstrate they are about Australia, not San Francisco:

  - From https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population/population-census/2021 :
    "The 2021 Census counted 25,422,788 people in Australia (excludes overseas visitors), an increase of 8.6 per cent since the 2016 Census." [source page: https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population/population-census/2021]

  - From https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population :
    "Australia’s population was 27,614,411 people at 30 June 2025." [source page: https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population]

  These verbatim quotes prove the visited pages are ABS (Australia) pages and do not contain San Francisco population statistics.

4) Candidate authoritative sources that the search/retrieval steps identified (these were found in the search/memory nodes but were NOT visited in this run). I include each candidate URL so you (or I, on a follow-up fetch) can extract the exact population figures and dates from them. I explicitly mark that these were identified but not visited, so no RAW_VISIT_CONTENT verbatim quote is available from these pages in this run.

  Government (official):
  - U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts: San Francisco city, California
    - URL found in the search results: https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/sanfranciscocitycalifornia
    - Note: this URL was returned by the search step in the run (see merged search result). It was not visited, so I do not have a verbatim quote from that page in the RAW VISIT CONTENT for this run.

  Major news outlet (examples found in memory/search results):
  - SF Standard article (example news outlet found in memory/search): "Population of San Francisco grew in 2023, Census data shows" — URL: https://sfstandard.com/2024/03/14/population-growth-san-francisco-2023-census/
  - Other possible news outlets to use (also returned in search/memory): San Francisco Chronicle, Reuters, Associated Press (not visited in this run). These need to be visited to extract the exact quoted population numbers and dates.

  Research institution / think tank (examples found in memory/search results):
  - Brookings Institution article: https://www.brookings.edu/articles/new-census-data-hints-at-an-urban-population-revival-assisted-by-immigration/
  - PPIC / Urban Institute / Brookings / Statista pages were present in retrieved memories as candidate research sources (not visited in this run).

  Important: all URLs above are from the run’s search/memory results (MERGED RESULTS / RETRIEVED MEMORIES). They are candidate authoritative sources but were not included in RAW VISIT CONTENT because they were not visited.

5) What is missing or conflicting (explicit statement required by the instructions)
- Missing: RAW_VISIT_CONTENT for any U.S. Census Bureau, San Francisco city/county government page, major news article (SF Chronicle, AP, Reuters, SF Standard, etc.), or research institution report that actually contains San Francisco population figures. Because those pages were not visited in this run, there is no verbatim text in RAW_VISIT_CONTENT that I can quote to prove the exact San Francisco population figures, nor their dates, nor the precise phrasing used on those pages.

- Conflicting: there is no direct conflict in RAW_VISIT_CONTENT because no San Francisco population figures were captured. The only content captured (ABS pages) is about Australia and therefore irrelevant to the Mandate.

6) Consequence for the Mandate
- I cannot fulfill the Mandate as requested (provide three authoritative population figures with dates and verbatim page quotes) because the run’s RAW_VISIT_CONTENT does not include any pages that report San Francisco population.

7) Exact steps I recommend (and can perform now if you want me to proceed) to complete the Mandate correctly
- I will fetch and extract verbatim quotes and exact values from the following three pages (one government, one major news outlet, one research institution). I will then cite each page with a direct URL and provide verbatim quotes (from RAW_VISIT_CONTENT) proving the population figure and the date. Please confirm you want me to proceed to visit these URLs and extract the numbers. The exact pages I propose to visit now are:
  1. U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts — San Francisco city, California: https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/sanfranciscocitycalifornia
     - What I will extract: the population figure shown (and the reference date the Census Bureau lists for that estimate), and the exact text/label (e.g., "Population estimates, July 1, 2023") as a verbatim quote.
  2. A major news outlet article reporting a recent San Francisco population estimate — e.g., SF Standard (https://sfstandard.com/2024/03/14/population-growth-san-francisco-2023-census/) or San Francisco Chronicle or Reuters (I can fetch multiple and use the most authoritative that explicitly reports a population number). 
     - What I will extract: the population figure quoted, the date of the estimate they quote, and the exact verbatim sentence.
  3. A research institution/think tank report or analysis that cites a San Francisco population figure — e.g., Brookings (https://www.brookings.edu/articles/new-census-data-hints-at-an-urban-population-revival-assisted-by-immigration/) or a PPIC or Urban Institute analysis.
     - What I will extract: the population figure and date they report, and the exact verbatim sentence.

- After extracting verbatim quotes from each page, I will:
  - Present the three figures (figure + date + direct quote + citation URL).
  - Identify any discrepancies between the three sources (exact numeric differences, difference in dates/reference periods, difference in geographic definition if any — city vs. city-and-county vs. metro area) with verbatim quotes showing how each source labels its figure.
  - Explain which figure is most likely the most accurate for "current population of San Francisco" and why (typically, U.S. Census Bureau official population estimates are primary for city population; news outlets may summarize or use the Census Bureau; research institution might use Census or a different mid-year estimate — I will show the reasons with quotes from the actual pages).

8) Execution trail and evidence notes from this run (transparency)
- Search node returned Census QuickFacts URL: https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/sanfranciscocitycalifornia (see merged search results).
- Visit nodes actually fetched: https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population/population-census/2021 and https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population (RAW VISIT CONTENT included and quoted above). These pages are about Australian national population and are not relevant to San Francisco.

9) Short answer (current status)
- Status: I did not extract San Francisco population figures in this run because the pages visited are ABS (Australia) pages, not San Francisco pages. Therefore I cannot currently provide the three requested population figures with verbatim page quotes. I can proceed immediately to fetch and extract the data from the candidate authoritative sources listed above (Census QuickFacts, a news article, a research institute article). Please confirm you want me to proceed, and I will visit each page, extract the exact population numbers, dates, and provide verbatim quotes and URLs as requested.

10) Appendices — verbatim quotes already obtained in this run (to show what the RAW_VISIT_CONTENT contains):
- "The 2021 Census counted 25,422,788 people in Australia (excludes overseas visitors), an increase of 8.6 per cent since the 2016 Census." — https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population/population-census/2021
- "Australia’s population was 27,614,411 people at 30 June 2025." — https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/people/population

End of report.

Next step (pick one):
- I will now visit the three candidate pages (U.S. Census QuickFacts for San Francisco, a chosen major news article that reports a population number, and a chosen research institution article) and extract exact population figures, dates, and verbatim quotes. Proceed? If yes, I will fetch and return the full requested deliverable (three sources with quoted evidence and discrepancy analysis).
final_output.action_summary: This run located candidate authoritative sources (e.g., U.S. Census QuickFacts for San Francisco) but the pages actually visited were Australian Bureau of Statistics pages (ABS). RAW VISIT CONTENT therefore does not contain any San Francisco population figures to quote. I cannot provide the three requested San Francisco population figures with verbatim page evidence from this run. I listed the exact candidate URLs to visit next (including https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/sanfranciscocitycalifornia) and requested permission to proceed to fetch those pages and extract the requested figures, quotes, dates, and discrepancy analysis.
